into LLM steps of like, okay, how do I approach this?

And so I started doing workshops and we had about 170 people come through that paid $500 to $1,000 to come essentially for a full day of major spring dumping.
And from that, that's when GrowthHive started, which was essentially people saying, hey, can you actually do this for me?
But then I think the realization was I...
I originally thought people wanted, like, custom workflows, essentially, right?

And what I realized is, like, what people need is the strategy and connecting the dots from them that then replicate the work of the best people possible, which are the ones setting the strategy, in a way that can be reproducible.
But then they also need the kind of the hands-on people that do the last mile as well, because things always get stuck in the last mile, right?
Where it's like, oh, I need to stage this in the CMS and hit publish.

And so we essentially built an AI coding agent that codifies work into code and then a runtime layer that runs that.

so anyways, that's kind of the TLDR of what we built underneath the hood.
